Off Calendar Jul-31-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SPRINGEL & FINK LLP, THOMAS JACK, DARREN EBNER's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement

Defendants Springel & Fink LLP, Thomas Jack, and Darren Ebner's unopposed Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

Good cause appearing, the court finds the March 18, 2025, settlement between Moving Defendants and Ciaran Harty is in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and 876.6 and under the analysis set out in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488.

This order shall have the effect provided by Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and otherwise provided by law for an order under sections 877 and 876.6.

Moving Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. (See Evidence Code section 452(d) [court records].) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "Although the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, the truth of statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable." (Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont Gen. Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
